title: Exception types should be public
description: Exception types should be "public"Analyze your codeintentionality-completemaintainabilityCode Smellerror-handlingapi-designWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoThe point of having custom exception types is to convey more information than is available in standard types. But custom exception types must bepublicfor that to work.If a method throws a non-public exception, the best you can do on the caller’s side is tocatchthe closestpublicbase of the class. However, you lose all the information that the new exception type carries.This rule will raise an issue if you directly inherit one of the following exception types in a non-public class:ExceptionSystemExceptionApplicationExceptionAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, B
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Non-public class inheriting from Exception, SystemException, or ApplicationException
logic.triggering_conditions: Class with internal, private, or protected modifier inheriting from Exception/SystemException/ApplicationException
logic.exclusion_conditions: Exclude public exception classes, non-exception classes, test code
logic.reasoning: Custom exception types must be public to convey their specialized information. If an exception is non-public (internal, private, protected), callers can only catch the closest public base type, losing all custom information. The rule detects class declarations inheriting from Exception, SystemException, or ApplicationException without 'public' modifier. Pattern matches class declarations with these base types and checks for non-public access modifiers (internal, private, protected) or absence of 'public'.

checks.message: Exception types should be public to convey specialized information to callers
